rationale: Grounded at 17d0b008ffd59f72015745749c2dfa4c2a0da002. The round-4 false-discharge finding is ADDRESSED: plan step 5(c) requires discharge only from the matched panel.Result's affirmative tally evidence, not a bare non-blocking gate result, and TestPanelRefuted_CrossRun_WarnPathDoesNotDischarge tables abandonment, missing-ref, and transient-gitErr while X@1 remains REQUEST_CHANGES, requiring Refuse, no discharge, and no close. G2's union hole is closed by read-union-write of every pending slot/round plus TestPanelRefuted_CrossRun_UnionReconcilesAll, which requires discharge X@1 and satisfy B@2 before close. The hatch hole is closed by pre-close reconciliation on env-skip, config-disabled, and abandoned paths, including fail-closed metadata reads. Thus every durable obligation is satisfied, affirmatively discharged, or refused. One non-blocking drafting note remains: step 4's explicit panelGate tuple omits the Result later required by step 5; implementation must extend that tuple (or an equivalent return struct).

findings.title: Warn passes cannot falsely discharge a carried RC
findings.detail: Plan 5(c) keys discharge to the matched panel.Result and explicitly refuses when abandonment, missing-ref, or transient-gitErr Warns while the tally still carries the pending slot's REQUEST_CHANGES. The named cross-run Warn-path table asserts no refutation_discharged write and no close.

findings.title: Pending obligations are unioned and fully reconciled
findings.detail: The marker path reads existing refutation_pending_entries, unions current applied slot/round pairs, writes fail-closed, and reconciles every uncovered entry. The X@1 plus B@2 cross-run test pins both discharge and satisfaction before close.

findings.title: Hatches bypass the gate but not durable-obligation reconciliation
findings.detail: The reconciliation is placed after the final blocking gate and before close on panel-present, no-panel, env-skip, config-disabled, and abandoned paths; unverifiable tally state or metadata read failure refuses completion.

findings.title: Config-disabled audit wording remains locally imprecise
findings.detail: Step 4 correctly says config-disabled writes no panel_gate_skipped audit, while step 5(e)'s singular statement that the hatch's own audit is still written can be read as covering both hatches. This does not weaken obligation reconciliation.

findings.title: Earlier panelGate signature paragraph was not refreshed for tally plumbing
findings.detail: Step 4 still specifies (*panel.Registration, []panel.Refutation, error), but step 5(c), spec R2/Scope, AC11, and the Warn regression require the matched panel.Result to be plumbed into reconciliation. Treat the later normative requirement as controlling and extend the tuple or use an equivalent result struct.

carried_forward: round-5 APPROVE carried: mechanism confirmed TOTAL/airtight; round-6 is a precision fix for F2 discharge-evidence acquisition (re-tally from panelReg.Dir) — no design/ADR/domain/AC change
